explanation: Foot, Yard, Inch, and Mile are all units of length historically derived from human-based measurements or ancient conventions, and are not part of the metric system. Meter is the base unit of length in the metric (SI) system.

Gunpowder, the compass, papermaking, and printing (specifically woodblock printing) are widely recognized as the Four Great Inventions of ancient China. An aqueduct is a significant engineering achievement of the Roman Empire, not China.
Pride (lions), School (fish), Gaggle (geese), and Parliament (owls) are all specific collective nouns for groups of animals. Constellation refers to a group of stars.
